content.problemStatement:
Alice has a string SSS of length NNN, consisting of lowercase English letters. She wants to remove a lexicographically minimal subsequence†^{\dagger}† TTT from SSS such that after removing TTT, the remaining characters of SSS cannot be rearranged to form a palindrome.

Your task is to help Alice by printing the subsequence TTT that satisfies these conditions. If no such subsequence TTT exists, print −1-1−1.

**Note:** The empty string is also considered a palindrome.The empty subsequence is lexicographically the smallest of all.

†^{\dagger}† A string TTT is called a **subsequence** of another string SSS if TTT can be derived from SSS by deleting zero or more characters without changing the order of the remaining characters.

String XXX is lexicographically smaller than string YYY, if either of the following satisfies:


XXX is a prefix of YYY and X≠YX \neq YX=Y.
There exists an index iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​ and Xj=Yj,∀jX_j = Y_j, \forall jXj​=Yj​,∀j such that 1≤j<i1 \leq j \lt i1≤j<i.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the length of string.
The next line contains string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case:



If a valid subsequence TTT exists, print the size of TTT on the first line.The subsequence TTT on the second line.
If no such subsequence TTT exists, print −1-1−1 in a single line.
constraints: SSS consists of lowercase english alphabets only.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We will choose the empty subsequence, as we cannot rearrange given SSS to form a palindrome.
Test case 222: No matter which subsequence you choose, it is still possible to rearrange SSS to form a palindrome.
Test case 333: We will choose the subsequence bababa, and remove it from SSS:

b‾baa‾→subsequence baba\underline{b}ba \underline{a}\xrightarrow{\text{subsequence } ba} ba b​baa​subsequence ba​ba, as we cannot rearrange bababa to form a palindrome.
